profile.name: Shuo Sun
profile.affiliation: Singapore-MIT Alliance for Research and Technology (SMART), M3S Interdisciplinary Research Group
profile.shortBio: Shuo Sun is a Postdoctoral Associate at SMART M3S whose research spans robotics, autonomous systems, and human-AI collective intelligence, with an emphasis on building intelligent systems that augment human potential and empower the workforce of the future.
profile.longBio: He received both his Ph.D. and B.Eng. degrees in Mechanical Engineering from the National University of Singapore, under the supervision of Professor Marcelo H. Ang Jr. His work bridges machine intelligence and human capability through robotics, autonomy, and collective intelligence, with a particular interest in systems that make human-AI collaboration more capable, meaningful, and deployable.

hero.bridgeStatement: Current work at SMART M3S focuses on human-AI collective intelligence and intelligent systems that augment people, while prior autonomy and robotics deployments provide the systems foundation underneath that shift.

home.researchSummary: The site foregrounds the present arc: from autonomous systems and motion planning toward intelligent systems that coordinate, support, and amplify human work.
home.missionTitle: Design intelligent systems that expand what people and teams can do.
home.contactPrompt: If you are working on robotics, autonomy, collective intelligence, AI-enabled labor, or adjacent human-centered systems research, feel free to reach out.
home.currentLensTitle: What the site should make obvious
home.currentLensBody: Shuo Sun is not only an autonomy researcher with older vehicle deployments. He is now positioned as a postdoctoral researcher connecting robotics and autonomous systems with broader questions about human-AI collaboration, augmentation, and the future of work.

publications.pageTitle: Publications across autonomy, robotics, simulation, and collective intelligence.
publications.pageLead: This archive brings together my work on robotics, autonomous systems, and newer research on human-AI collective intelligence and AI-enabled work.

projectsPage.pageTitle: Projects that show how ideas become deployed systems.
projectsPage.pageLead: From autonomous vehicles and robotic platforms to current human-AI research directions, this page is meant to help visitors quickly understand both technical depth and research direction.

cv.contactNote: Email is the best way to reach out about research collaboration, talks, postdoctoral opportunities, or applied robotics and AI systems work.
cv.pageLead: A structured academic profile generated from the same data that powers the website, so updates to publications, projects, appointments, and contact details stay aligned.

researchThemes.title: Human-AI Collective Intelligence
researchThemes.description: Developing intelligent systems that help people and organizations think, coordinate, and work more effectively together.

researchThemes.title: Robotics and Autonomous Systems
researchThemes.description: Building real-world robotic and autonomous platforms that can perceive, plan, and operate safely in structured and semi-structured environments.

researchThemes.title: Planning, Prediction, and Simulation
researchThemes.description: Studying motion planning, trajectory generation, prediction, and generative simulation as the algorithmic backbone for capable intelligent systems.

timeline.title: Postdoctoral Associate, SMART M3S
timeline.summary: Current research at the intersection of robotics, autonomous systems, and human-AI collective intelligence.

timeline.title: Ph.D., Mechanical Engineering, NUS
timeline.summary: Research on autonomous systems, motion planning, prediction, and simulation under Professor Marcelo H. Ang Jr.

timeline.title: B.Eng., Mechanical Engineering, NUS
timeline.summary: Foundation in engineering design, robotics, and interdisciplinary systems work.

projects.title: Human-AI Work Intelligence
projects.summary: Current work on intelligent systems that augment human capability, support collective intelligence, and help shape more meaningful AI-enabled labor.
contributions: Exploring how robotics and AI systems can move from autonomy-only optimization toward human-centered augmentation.
Connecting technical system design with questions of collaboration, workforce empowerment, and collective performance.
Building on prior systems and autonomy research to inform broader human-AI environments.

projects.title: Autonomous Mini-bus
projects.summary: Development of an autonomous mini-bus platform for on-demand shuttle services in geofenced environments.
contributions: Led technical design across hardware layout, sensing, and autonomous driving software.
Worked across perception, HD mapping, localization, planning, and control as an integrated stack.
Extended deployment-oriented autonomy beyond simulation into operational service scenarios.

projects.title: Autonomous Road Sweeper
projects.summary: A full autonomous cleaning platform for mixed indoor and outdoor environments, from drive-by-wire retrofitting to full-stack software.
contributions: Led the development of the autonomous road sweeper prototype as the project lead.
Built a complete autonomy stack spanning perception, HD map, localization, planning, and control.
Transferred the prototype and software system to the Kärcher Robotics team for commercialization.

projects.title: DriveSceneGen
projects.summary: A generative simulation system that produces diverse and realistic driving scenarios from scratch for autonomous vehicle development and evaluation.
contributions: Helped push research beyond downstream planning toward data generation and scenario diversity.
Connected generative modeling with the needs of safety validation and realistic autonomy development.
Released code and project materials alongside the publication.

projects.title: Autonomous Golf Buggies
projects.summary: A fleet autonomy project for mobility-on-demand in an intra-fab environment, combining drive-by-wire design, software architecture, planning, control, and onboard interfaces.
contributions: Led a seven-intern team and designed the overall software architecture.
Built drive-by-wire and the autonomy hardware stack from scratch.
Implemented perception, HD map representation, trajectory planning, model predictive control, and user interface components.
